en: Working On, Over, or Near Water

Drowning-risk work arrangements must include the availability of life-saving PPE and rescue equipment. Watercraft equipment must provide one life-saving device per person.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 106(1), 107(1)

Worksite staffing must include trained users of rescue equipment. At least one person with resuscitation knowledge must be present on-site.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 106(1-2)

Before commencing work, employees must be instructed in the handling of protective and rescue equipment and in the correct procedures in the event of accidents.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 106(2)
Annual water rescue drills must occur for all employees involved, simulating realistic emergency scenarios, with documented records.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 106(2)
High-risk water work arrangements must include manned motorboats equipped and crewed for rescue operations\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 106(3)
